title: Direct Labor Variances
event: Reyes Co. paid 2,100 actual direct labor hours at $15.50 per hour. Standards allow 2,200 hours at $16.00 for the output achieved. Compute the rate and efficiency variances.

computationPaths.narration: Same architecture as materials, relabeled: rate replaces price, efficiency replaces quantity — and this time both come in Favorable.

memorize.body: Rate variance = AH × (AR − SR). Efficiency variance = SR × (AH − SH).
Cheaper than standard and faster than standard = both Favorable. Always attach the direction label — a number alone is half an answer.

questions.prompt: AH 2,100, AR $15.50, SR $16.00, SH 2,200. Labor EFFICIENCY variance = ?

distractors.feedback: You valued the 100 saved hours at the ACTUAL rate. Efficiency uses the STANDARD rate: 16.00 × 100 = 1,600 F.
That's the RATE variance. Efficiency measures the hours saved: 100 hrs × $16 = 1,600 F.
